title: Beam Global (BEEM) Q2 2025 Earnings - Margin Expansion and Geographic Growth
text: Beam Global Q2 2025 Financial Performance: Revenue was $7.1 million in Q2 2025, representing a 12% sequential increase from Q1. Gross Margin reached 20% GAAP; 30% adjusted excluding noncash depreciation. Net Loss was $4.3 million, improved from $4.9 million in Q2 2024. Operating Expenses were $5.9 million, down $1.2 million (17%) year-over-year. Cash Position was $3.4 million at quarter-end, up from $2.5 million; company remains debt-free with unused $100 million credit line. International Revenue reached 37% of 2025 revenues versus 15% in 2024. Commercial Sales represented 60% of year-to-date revenue from nongovernment entities. Order Backlog was $7 million as of Q2 2025 close. The company achieved the best GAAP gross profit margin in its history while expanding globally through initiatives like establishing Beam Middle East. Headwinds included a $11 million goodwill impairment charge and declining federal government sales, which previously represented a substantial revenue source.
